title: Hamilton Beach Electric Burr Coffee Grinder with Large 16oz Hopper & 18 Settings For 2-14 Cups, Stainless Steel (80385)
description:
About this item
18 PRECISION GRIND SETTINGS FOR EVERYTHING FROM ESPRESSO TO FRENCH PRESS. With the Hamilton Beach Burr Grinder, you can easily change the texture of coffee grounds from extra-fine to coarse with just a twist of the dial. Great for herbs and spices too.
GRIND THE AMOUNT YOU NEED WITH 2-14 CUP SELECTOR. Whether you’re brewing coffee for just yourself or a group of friends, this electric coffee grinder makes it simple to customize the amount of grounds each time you grind whole coffee beans.
MESS-FREE POURING: The grounds container on the coffee bean grinder has a snap-on lid that lets you easily pour ground coffee into your brew basket without spills.
TOP BURR REMOVES FOR EASY CLEANING. With a few quick turns, the top grinding wheel removes for cleaning. Soft bristle brush included. The containers and lids are dishwasher safe.
LARGE BEAN HOPPER. Store two full cups of coffee beans in the large beans hopper with lid. It keeps your beans fresh and ready to grind.
reviews_5s:
I am on a coffee adventure! Brewing, frothing, and now, thanks to this little grinder, grinding!
It looks great! Works great! Plug and play right out of the box! I primarily use a Moka pot to make my coffee and with a Moka pot, the grind matters. I have been more than happy with the performance of this little grinder!
Consistent grind! Not too loud!
If you’re looking for a decently priced grinder, grab this one!My favorite coffee is a medium roast Sumatra sold at my local grocer in whole bean. Unfortunately, the store grinders get used for flavored coffee more than unflavored, so my rich, mellow Sumatra tastes like some awful vanilla pecan cimmamon french roast nonsense. Trashcan punch. I had previously owned the Cuisinart burr grinder for several years. It ground pretty well but was unbelievably loud and very messy.

This Hamilton Beach grinder is surprisingly good given the very low price. A quick rundown. It is small-- probably half the size of my old grinder. It sits neatly on the bar above my brewers in the space where I used to keep my k-cups. It is much quieter. It is still loud enough to startle the dog, but not loud enough that it wakes the whole house if I use it in the morning. The grind is very consistent. I've been using a fine setting (espresso) and there have been no bigger bits in the grind so far. It's cleaner. Static cling and ground coffee still present a challenge, but coffee doesn't explode from the cup when I open it like the Cuisinart did. Think "occasional cleanup" not "steel yourself for the mess" because you want fresh grounds.
My pod machine died and I replaced it with the Hamilton Beach Scoop single serve drip machine. This grinder is a fantastic companion to that machine. I can make one GOOD cup at a time. With the price for the combination of the drip machine and the grinder below that of most of the other brand name grinders, this is a very good buy.I bought this for my mother-in-law and she’s absolutely obsessed with it. She likes the freshness of beans being ground right before the coffee is being made so it’s a winner and I highly recommend it plus it doesn’t take a whole lot of space on your counter so it’s great.It is really worth every penny grind coffee quite well my only complaint is you need to make sure the selection container is pushed all the way back or it makes a mess otherwise it is a very good machine.After reading many reviews, I decided against the small cheaper ones. I took a chance on this one and it’s great! Maybe a little noisy but we don’t mind, it’s doing its job perfectly. No mess and we love the different adjustment settings. The size is small and compact. Highly recommendMuy excelenteReplaced one that wore out after many years of use. Works well & doesn’t take up much room on shelfEasy to use, easy to clean with a small footprint.My Cuisinart Grinder just quite and hard as I tried I guess it just died. This is probably the 3rd Cuisinart coffee grinder I've had so I decided to try another brand and didn't want to spend $60.00. Reviews on this Hamilton Beach were good and the price was better than I thought.. So far I am really happy with it. Lots of settings and it's not as noisy as the Cuisinart grinder. I like it!!Tuve una versión más pequeña de la misma marca y esta la supera en capacidad, facilidad de uso y limpieza
reviews_1s:
Very disappointed with this Hamilton Beach grinder. I love my other Hamilton Beach appliances but in this case they put their brand on a common cheap imported grinder that you can find under many no-name brands. The hopper to hold the grinds is very difficult to insert and remove, and the grinder safety switch is poorly designed so it will start grinding when the hopper is not completely inserted. Even if it is completely inserted it makes a mess and some of the grounds end up outside the chamber.

On top of that, the grind is very uneven - see the picture - this is set to the middle of the range for a drip grind. The grounds are not even at all and every time it spits out a big chunk of coffee that is dust like espresso grind.Noisy. Really noisy. Not coffee grinding noisy, but motor bearing noisy. But something I can live with.
No. 1 problem is that it creates a coffee grounds mess on the counter. The product description shows how you can pour the grounds out of the grinder cup “no mess!”. But the hole is too small for that. You have to take the lid off, and the coffee grounds stuck to the underside of the lid fall to the counter. The grinding wheel and the plastic construction creates static electricity build-up between the grounds and the cup. This causes the grounds to adhere to the inside of the plastic cup, so to get them free, you have to tap the cup on the counter and grounds are then everywhere. The static is so bad, that the grounds don’t even pour out when dumping into the filter, so again, you’re tapping the bottom of the cup like a bottle of ketchup, and stray grounds are once again, everywhere. I’d return it, but I’m past the deadline. I’ve tried every technique I can think of over the past two months. Can’t get the grounds to come out clean. It’s going into the trash and I’m going back to the simple Mr. Coffee grinder that this one was supposed to replace.This thing was possessed. It would turn itself on at random time of the day so that I had to unplug it everytime I wanted to use it which was super inconvenient the way my coffee station is set up. Then it finally just died all together. I had it for only 4 months. Stupid thing. pass on this.This thing is a complete waste of money. I cannot get an espresso grind to save my soul. I’ve went through all the troubleshooting steps to no avail. If you like coarser grind, it might be ok. The static is horrible and I’ve wasted more coffee than I have brewed. What a disappointment!I tried using this grinder for one bag of coffee beans and it stopped working. The grinder felt hot and I had to let it cool down for it to start grinding again. Seems to work on a small batch for your morning coffee but not much else. Outside of the return policy so Amazon won’t let me return it.I used this Hamilton Beach grinder once a day for a month. It stopped working and smelled burnt.
Don’t waste your money on this one.It ground a few coffee beans then it got stuck and stopped working. The video showed it could hold lots of beans but in reality we could only add like a tbsp at a time or it would get stuck easily, made loud noise but no ground coffee coming out and we had to remove the beans and clean inside multiple times to grind a couple cups. It takes lots of time for us to grind just a few beans at a time so it's not worth it. I will look for a different machine with better design. Not recommend this!Have had this for 3 months, dosen't grind the Coffee. Have to press the grind button 10 times just to get 1 fill of the plastic container.
No support from the companyIt’s loud, the settings don’t make any difference in grind, and it doesn’t grind consistently. I’ve had to shake it while it’s grinding to “help it.” I wish it wasn’t past the point of return (I got busy) because this thing is a poor excuse for a grinder. I’m pretty sure grinding coffee with a rock would be more efficient!Hamilton Beach, how could you put such a messy product, much less effective than your smaller model selling for $18? OK, got that off my chest.

The plastics used cause the coffee to stick to the harvest lid and cup, body of the grinder, impossible to empty without dropping coffee everywhere. We used a LARGE plastic box to catch the coffee, and THE COFFEE DIDN'T STICK TO THE BOX! So how did they not build something with a plastic rejecting the polarity of coffee grounds?

The amount of ground coffee is not much when grinding for long time use instead of daily, you'll spend more time cleaning, emptying, than grinding. We've used their smaller portable grinder for more than several years, wanted to upgrade for volume.

This wasn't an upgrade. Going back to Hamilton Beach Fresh Grind 4.5oz until we find something better.
